lessons.title: Your First Webpage
lessons.summary: Write HTML tags that browsers understand.

payload.title: Tags are HTML's building blocks
payload.body:
HTML uses tags like

to tell the browser what to show. An opening tag

and closing tag

wrap the content.

payload.prompt: What does <h1> create on a webpage?
payload.explanation: <h1> is the largest heading tag — it makes text big and bold.
Closing tags have a / before the tag name.

lessons.title: Paragraphs of Text
lessons.summary:
Use

to add paragraphs to your page.

payload.title: The paragraph tag
payload.body:
Wrap any block of text in

and

to make a paragraph. Browsers add a little space above and below automatically.

payload.prompt: Which tag is used for a paragraph of text?
Wrap this sentence in a paragraph tag.

lessons.title: Clickable Links
lessons.summary: Use <a href> to link to another page.

payload.title: Anchor tags make links
payload.body: The <a> tag creates a link. The href attribute tells the browser where to go when clicked.

payload.prompt: Which attribute holds the URL in an <a> tag?
payload.explanation: href stands for Hypertext REFerence — the destination address.
href is the attribute that holds the link destination.

lessons.title: Adding Images
lessons.summary: Use to show pictures on your page.

payload.title: Images with
payload.body: is a self-closing tag — it doesn't need . The src attribute points to the image file, and alt describes it for screen readers.

payload.prompt: What does the src attribute in do?
payload.explanation: src is short for source — it tells the browser where to find the image.

payload.prompt: Add the correct attribute to show an image.
payload.explanation: src tells the browser where the image lives.

lessons.title: Building Lists
lessons.summary: Use <ul> and <li> to make bullet lists.

payload.title: Unordered lists
payload.body: <ul> creates a bullet list. Each item goes inside <li> (list item) tags. Use <ol> instead of <ul> for numbered lists.

payload.explanation: li = list item. It goes inside <ul> or <ol>.

lessons.title: Style with CSS
lessons.summary: Change colors and sizes with CSS.

payload.title: CSS makes pages beautiful
payload.body: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets. You can write CSS inside a <style> tag and target HTML elements by name. Use color to change text color.

payload.explanation: CSS controls colors, fonts, sizes, spacing — everything visual.

payload.prompt: Pick the CSS property that changes text color.
payload.explanation: The color property sets the text color of an element.
